item.title: Bob Dylan on what New York City means to him | 60 Minutes Archive
item.description: In 2004, Bob Dylan spoke with 60 Minutes' Ed Bradley about the role New York City played in his life. In this expanded clip from the interview outtakes, the singer-songwriter discusses his journey from Minnesota to New York. Today, the musician turns 85.

item.title: Second Ebola treatment center set on fire in epicenter of disease's outbreak
item.description: Angry residents of a town at the epicenter of the Ebola outbreak in eastern Congo​ attacked and burned a tent that was part of a health center where people are being treated for the virus.

item.title: Gunman killed after opening fire on Secret Service checkpoint outside White House
item.description: A bystander was also wounded, but no Secret Service officers were injured, officials said. President Trump was in the White House at the time of the incident.

item.title: California Gov. Newsom declares state of emergency for Orange County chemical leak as DA launches probe into its cause
item.description: Southern California officials are desperately searching for a safe resolution to a leaking toxic chemical tank at an aerospace facility.

item.title: Why Kouri Richins jurors decided Utah mom was a killer
item.description: At Kouri Richins' sentencing for the murder of Eric Richins, her husband and father of their three sons, she declared her innocence to the court and to her children. But for jurors on the case, the evidence told a different story.

item.title: "48 Hours" show schedule
item.description: True crime. Real justice. To miss it would be a crime.

item.title: Why did Kouri Richins want her husband dead?
item.description: Kouri Richins published a children's book to help her sons cope with the loss of their father – then she was convicted of Eric Richins' murder.

item.title: Iran moving World Cup training base from U.S. to Mexico
item.description: Iran had been scheduled to train in Tucson, Arizona, but it will now move its training base to Tijuana, Mexico, just south of San Diego.

item.title: Peace deal with Iran has been "largely negotiated," Trump says
item.description: Following a call with several Middle Eastern leaders, President Trump said that a peace deal with Iran had been "largely negotiated."

item.title: Russia launches heavy missile strikes on Kyiv after Ukraine drone attack
item.description: A large ballistic missile attack pounded Kyiv, authorities said, wounding at least five people after Moscow threatened retaliation for strikes in Russian-occupied eastern Ukraine.

item.title: Kyle Busch's cause of death revealed by family
item.description: Kyle Busch, who won more races in NASCAR's top three series than anyone in history, died suddenly on Thursday.

item.title: What to know about California chemical tank set to explode or leak
item.description: The tank at GKN Aerospace is estimated to contain 7,000 gallons of methyl methacrylate, a volatile chemical used to produce plastics.

item.title: Passengers from high Ebola risk countries can enter Atlanta, Houston and D.C. airports
item.description: Officials have increased the number of countries in Africa at risk for Ebola from three to 10. A third entry point is open for Americans traveling from certain high-risk countries back into the U.S. Holly Williams reports.

item.title: Trump says he is close to an Iran deal and reopening Strait of Hormuz, but Iran disagrees
item.description: President Trump announced that he is close to reaching an agreement with Iran to end the conflict and reopen the Strait of Hormuz, but Iran is dismissing his assertion. Imtiaz Tyab reports.

item.title: Tank leaking hazardous chemicals in Southern California brings risk of explosion
item.description: More than 50,000 people have been evacuated due to a failing industrial tank filled with an extremely hazardous chemical that is leaking from an aerospace facility in Southern California, and it could explode. The governor has declared a state of emergency. Lana Zak reports.

item.title: Kyle Busch died of "severe pneumonia that progressed into sepsis," family says
item.description: NASCAR star Kyle Busch's family on Saturday revealed his cause of death as "severe pneumonia that progressed into sepsis, resulting in rapid and overwhelming associated complications." Lindsey Reiser reports.

item.title: Possible gunshots heard from White House: CBS News reporters
item.description: Several shots were heard from the White House property on Saturday. Aaron Navarro reports from the location after Secret Service told him to get down immediately.

item.title: 5/23: CBS Weekend News
item.description: Shots fired near the White House; thousands evacuate Southern California toxic leak zone.

item.title: Trump says U.S. is "getting a lot closer" to agreement with Iran
item.description: U.S. officials and negotiators were discussing a process to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, sources briefed on the conversations said.

item.title: "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan" guests for May 24, 2026
item.description: This week's guests include Sen. Chris Van Hollen, White House National Economic Council Director Kevin Hassett, and former White House Coronavirus Task Force Coordinator Dr. Deborah Birx.

item.title: Serbian protesters clash with police amid anti-government rally in Belgrade
item.description: Protesters on Saturday streamed into central Belgrade, many carrying banners and wearing T-shirts inscribed with the "Students win" motto of the youth movement.

item.title: Ebola kills 3 Red Cross workers in the Congo, organization says
item.description: The three workers, who fell ill in March and died in May, were "among the first known victims of the outbreak," the Red Cross said.

item.title: Trump administration to require most green card applicants to leave U.S. first
item.description: The Trump administration on Friday announced a sweeping policy designed to make it harder for immigrants already in the U.S. to get permanent residency.

item.title: Over 40,000 evacuated in California chemical leak as officials warn tank may explode
item.description: Southern California officials have expanded evacuations in several Orange County cities due to concerns over a leaking toxic chemical tank that they say is "actively in crisis."

item.title: Coal mine explosion in China kills at least 82 people, state media say
item.description: The incident marks China's deadliest mining accident in years.

item.title: Teen's mystery illness solved: "It took 18 years to get an answer"
item.description: Lucia Adarve had a long list of symptoms that no one could diagnose. Her mother Lisa was determined to find an answer.

item.title: Kouri Richins: Behind the Facade
item.description: Scared of their own mother. Why the children of convicted killer Kouri Richins want her to stay behind bars. "48 Hours" correspondent Natalie Morales reports.

item.title: SpaceX launches more powerful Super Heavy-Starship rocket
item.description: Perfecting SpaceX's mammoth rocket will be critical to NASA's plans for returning astronauts to the surface of the moon.

item.title: How some U.S. schools are teaching kindness classes
item.description: Kindness 101 is a program that uses the stories CBS News finds in our "On the Road" series to teach kindness and character. The stories are paired with lesson plans created by a nonprofit at Iowa's Drake University called "Character Counts!"

item.title: This factor may determine whether you get rich. You don't control it.
item.description: Landing a good-paying job may not be enough to buy a home. A new study finds family wealth plays an outsized role in who becomes a homeowner.
